title: Products of nilpotent groups
author_string: Amberg, Bernhard, Franciosi, Silvana, Giovanni, Francesco De
abstract:
Abstract
               In the early 1950s finite products of two nilpotent groups were considered by several authors, and the conjecture arose that these groups were always soluble (Huppert 1953; Ito 1951; Szep 1951; Wielandt 1951). This seemed to be an unusually difficult problem, even for products of two abelian groups. However, in 1955 Ito, using a surprisingly short commutator calculation, obtained the following famous result, which even holds for arbitrary groups.
